output.summary: Review of the Form block against block-type documentation (form.md) and accessibility patterns (landmarks.md). The block is largely well-implemented but has several gaps: (1) Missing landmark accessibility -- the form region should use a semantic <form> element or explicit role='form' with an accessible name via aria-labelledby; the outer <section> lacks an aria-labelledby pointing to its heading. (2) No Form.css file exists for Gravity Forms style overrides (required by section 7 of form.md). (3) The gap-y value in TSX/PHP is gap-y-10 but the block-type doc specifies gap-y-16 -- needs alignment. (4) No test page exists yet. (5) The block does not handle a missing Gravity Forms plugin gracefully on the frontend (checklist item). (6) The sticky heading column is missing self-start class in TSX (present in PHP but missing from TSX className).

fileChanges.description: Add landmark accessibility to form region and outer section
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-labelledby on the outer <section> pointing to an auto-generated ID derived from the heading text (e.g., using sanitize_title or wp_unique_id). 2. Wrap the Gravity Forms output in a container that has role='form' and aria-labelledby pointing to the heading, providing the landmark pattern required by form.md. 3. Add graceful fallback when Gravity Forms plugin is not active -- check if function_exists('gravity_form') or shortcode_exists('gravityform') before rendering, and output nothing (or a comment) if missing. 4. Fix gap-y value from gap-y-10 to gap-y-16 to match block-type doc specification.

fileChanges.description: Add landmark accessibility to editor markup and fix gap-y mismatch
fileChanges.details: 1. Add aria-labelledby on the outer <section> element using a generated ID from clientId. 2. Give the heading an id attribute so it can be referenced. 3. Fix gap-y value from gap-y-10 to gap-y-16 to match block-type doc specification. 4. The sticky heading div already has self-start in the className -- verify this is correct (it IS present in TSX via the cn object).

fileChanges.description: Create CSS file for Gravity Forms style overrides
fileChanges.details: Per form.md section 7 (Form Element Styling), create a Form.css file with CSS overrides for Gravity Forms elements. Include: 1. Text input and select styling (border-radius, border-color, background, min-height, focus states). 2. Checkbox and radio styling (border-radius, checked background color, size). 3. Textarea styling (border-radius, min-height). 4. Submit button hover state. 5. Use proper CSS specificity to override Gravity Forms default styles -- avoid @layer, use careful specificity. These styles should be verified against the actual Figma design (which is not available in this planning session, so mark as needs-design-verification).

fileChanges.description: No changes needed to attributes -- all required attributes are present
fileChanges.details: Current attributes (anchor, eyebrow, heading, description, buttons, headingPosition, formId) match the block-type doc exactly. No attribute changes required.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: Form landmark: The form region should be identifiable as a 'form' landmark with an accessible name (aria-labelledby pointing to the heading) so assistive technology users can navigate to it.
accessibilityGaps.currentState: The form is rendered inside a plain <div class='form-section-form'> with no role='form' and no accessible name. The outer <section> element has no aria-labelledby.
accessibilityGaps.requiredChange: In Form.php: (1) Add an id attribute to the heading element (via ThemeHeading or a wrapper) so it can be referenced. (2) Add aria-labelledby on the <section> pointing to the heading id, making it a proper region landmark. (3) Add role='form' and aria-labelledby (pointing to the heading) on the div wrapping the Gravity Forms shortcode output OR use a <form> element if possible (though Gravity Forms renders its own <form>, so role='form' on a wrapper div may conflict -- use aria-label instead for the form container describing its purpose). In Form.tsx: Apply matching ARIA attributes to editor markup.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: When a <section> is used as a page section, it should have an accessible name via aria-labelledby to be a proper 'region' landmark.
accessibilityGaps.currentState: The outer <section> has no aria-labelledby. It is an unnamed section landmark.
accessibilityGaps.requiredChange: Add an auto-generated id to the heading rendered by ThemeHeading, then reference it with aria-labelledby on the <section>. In PHP, use wp_unique_id('form-heading-') to generate unique ids. In TSX, use clientId-based id.

accessibilityGaps.requirement: All interactive form elements must have visible focus indicators.
accessibilityGaps.currentState: No Form.css exists to ensure focus indicators are visible on Gravity Forms elements. The Gravity Forms plugin may or may not provide adequate focus styling.
accessibilityGaps.requiredChange: Create Form.css with explicit focus-visible styles for inputs, selects, textareas, checkboxes, radios, and the submit button. Ensure focus borders use the accent color.

currentIssues: No test page exists for the Form block (search for 'test-form' returned 0 results)
No test page content file existed in the state directory until this planning session created one
requiredVariations: Beside Layout - Full Content: All fields populated, sticky heading, form rendered
Beside Layout - Minimal: Only heading and formId, tests shouldDisplay() for eyebrow/description/buttons
Above Layout - Full Content: Centered stacked layout with all fields
Above Layout - Minimal: Centered layout with minimal content
Long Content - Beside Layout: Edge case with very long heading/description for overflow/wrapping/sticky testing
No Form Selected: Tests empty formId state -- form area should not render
Empty Optional Fields: Only heading set, no other optional fields

designDecisions.question: The block-type doc specifies gap-y-16 but the current implementation uses gap-y-10. Which is correct?
designDecisions.recommendation: Use gap-y-16 as specified in the block-type doc (form.md section 3). The doc is the authoritative source for layout values. Update both TSX and PHP.

designDecisions.question: Should the form container div have role='form' given that Gravity Forms already renders a <form> element inside it?
designDecisions.recommendation: Do NOT add role='form' to the wrapper div since Gravity Forms renders its own <form> element inside. Adding role='form' on a parent div would create a nested/duplicate form landmark. Instead, rely on Gravity Forms' own <form> element for the form landmark. The outer <section> should get aria-labelledby pointing to the heading to serve as a named 'region' landmark. This satisfies the landmarks pattern requirement without creating accessibility issues from duplicate landmarks.

designDecisions.question: How should the heading id be generated for aria-labelledby?
designDecisions.recommendation: In PHP: use wp_unique_id('form-heading-') to generate a unique id and pass it to ThemeHeading. In TSX: use `form-heading-${clientId}` as the id. ThemeHeading may need to accept an optional headingId prop, OR the id can be set on the wrapper div containing ThemeHeading.

designDecisions.question: Form.css does not exist -- should it be created with Gravity Forms overrides even without Figma designs to reference?
designDecisions.recommendation: Create a minimal Form.css with sensible defaults (consistent with theme tokens) for focus states, border-radius, and basic input styling. Mark it as 'needs-design-verification' so it can be refined when Figma designs are available. At minimum, ensure focus-visible states are styled with the accent color, which is a hard accessibility requirement.

designDecisions.question: Should the block handle a missing Gravity Forms plugin gracefully?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes, per the form.md checklist item 'Handles missing form plugin gracefully'. In Form.php, add a check before rendering the shortcode: if the gravityform shortcode is not registered, skip the form output div entirely (same as when formId is empty). This prevents broken shortcode text from appearing on the frontend.

variations.demoDescription: Two-column layout with heading on the left (sticky) and form on the right. All ThemeHeading fields populated.
Only heading and form, no eyebrow, description, or buttons. Tests empty state handling with shouldDisplay().
Stacked centered layout with all fields populated. Max-width constrained.
Stacked layout with only heading, no optional fields. Tests centered alignment with minimal content.
Edge case: very long heading and description to test overflow, wrapping, and sticky behavior with excessive content.
Tests the empty form state when no formId is set. The form area should not render, but the heading content should still display.
Only the required heading attribute set, no eyebrow, description, or buttons. Tests shouldDisplay() for all optional fields.
